findings.title: Inadequate Consent Mechanism
findings.description: The current consent mechanism does not meet GDPR requirements for granularity, leading to potential legal compliance issues and user distrust.
assumptions: Users require clear options for opting into data tracking.

evidence.excerpt: The current consent mechanism is inadequate, as it does not meet GDPR requirements for granularity.

recommendations: Revise the consent mechanism to ensure it meets GDPR requirements.

metadata.mitigation: Implement a revised consent mechanism that provides users with clear options for opting into data tracking.

findings.title: GDPR Compliance Audit Required
findings.description: A full GDPR compliance audit is necessary to address legal concerns regarding data processing, consent mechanisms, and data retention policies, blocking the EU market launch.
assumptions: Compliance with GDPR is essential for market entry in the EU.

evidence.excerpt: Conduct a full GDPR compliance audit to address legal concerns regarding data processing.

recommendations: Conduct a full GDPR compliance audit before proceeding.

metadata.mitigation: Perform a comprehensive GDPR compliance audit.

findings.title: Privacy Risks in User Tracking
findings.description: There are significant privacy risks associated with user behavior tracking, particularly regarding the potential for re-identification from anonymized data shared with advertisers.
assumptions: Anonymized data can still pose risks of re-identification.

evidence.excerpt: There are significant privacy risks associated with user behavior tracking.

recommendations: Implement stronger anonymization techniques and user controls.

metadata.mitigation: Enhance anonymization techniques and provide users with more control over their data.

findings.title: Authentication Weaknesses Detected
findings.description: The authentication flow may have weaknesses that could lead to unauthorized access, particularly in the context of user behavior tracking.
assumptions: Existing authentication mechanisms may not be robust enough.

evidence.excerpt: Detected auth risk keywords in 3 source(s).

metadata.mitigation: Conduct a security review of the authentication flow and implement necessary improvements.

findings.title: Compliance Risks Identified
findings.description: There are compliance risks related to GDPR and CCPA due to inadequate data handling practices and lack of transparency.
assumptions: Current data handling practices may not align with regulatory requirements.

evidence.excerpt: Detected compliance risk keywords in 5 source(s).

recommendations: Enhance data handling practices to ensure compliance with GDPR and CCPA.

metadata.mitigation: Review and improve data handling practices to align with GDPR and CCPA requirements.

summary: Identified 5 risks (2 compliance, 2 privacy, 1 security). 2 flagged as blockers. Risk gate: FAILED (3 blockers, 2 warnings).
